title: How I Spent My Summer Vacation

jokes.text: Speaking of prisoners, let's talk about Will.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil wants to talk about Will after coming inside
jokes.punchline: Equating Will to a prisoner with the transition phrase
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay transition that establishes Phil's attitude toward Will while being economical and character-specific

jokes.text: Switzerland. / Now that that's settled, I'll go fire up the Jacuzzi.
jokes.setup: Philip suggesting a school 'further away' than usual
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's Switzerland, then immediately acts like it's decided
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation joke with perfect timing - the casual dismissiveness makes it funnier

jokes.text: Would you consider Great Britain? / Well, a man can dream, can't he?
jokes.setup: Philip apologizes for suggesting Switzerland
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests another foreign country, then defends his right to dream
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation that shows Phil's persistence in wanting Will gone

jokes.text: Madam, I went to boarding school. And then I went to therapy.
jokes.setup: Philip asks Geoffrey to tell about his boarding school experience
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's deadpan revelation about needing therapy afterward
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expectations with perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery and economical structure

jokes.text: Who asked you? Get my lunch. / Right away, sir. / The man has a tapeworm.
jokes.setup: Philip dismisses Geoffrey's therapy comment rudely
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's muttered insult about Philip's eating habits
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey sass moment that shows their dynamic while getting in a weight joke

jokes.text: Does James Brown have a perm?
jokes.setup: Philip asks if Vivian thinks she can manipulate him with sex
jokes.punchline: Vivian's rhetorical question implying an obvious yes
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference that's both economical and delivers a clear message about their relationship dynamic

jokes.text: Oh, Lord, I don't know whether to dive under the table or do the running man.
jokes.setup: Vivian's James Brown reference and Phil's realization about manipulation
jokes.punchline: Phil's exaggerated reaction mixing fear with dance reference
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy setup with period-appropriate dance reference

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, you look great, man. You look like you lost some... hair.
jokes.setup: Will compliments Uncle Phil on looking great and losing weight
jokes.punchline: Switches from expected 'weight' to 'hair' for an insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Will misdirection joke that establishes his roasting style immediately upon return

jokes.text: Looks like the whole Jackson family could live in those pants.
jokes.setup: Will is wearing oversized pants from Philadelphia
jokes.punchline: Philip's exaggerated comment about the pants' size using Jackson family reference
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor about 90s fashion trends with good visual

jokes.text: No wonder Willy Williams moved to LA.
jokes.setup: Hilary explains that's how brothers dress in Philly
jokes.punchline: Philip's made-up explanation for why someone left Philadelphia
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist humor that shows Philip's out-of-touch perspective on street fashion

jokes.text: Will told everyone at the airport I was Bryant Gumbel.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains what happened at the airport
jokes.punchline: Reveals Will's lie about Carlton's identity
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference joke that plays on Carlton's preppy appearance vs. Bryant Gumbel

jokes.text: I thought I'd get my luggage faster. / No, five old ladies attacked me for being mean to Willard.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains his reasoning for going along with Will's lie
jokes.punchline: Reveals the unexpected consequence - being attacked by elderly viewers
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that subverts expectations about celebrity recognition with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: Daddy, I need $500. / Hil, you should really consider expanding your vocabulary a little.
jokes.setup: Hilary repeats the exact same request for money
jokes.punchline: Will points out her limited vocabulary/one-track mind
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor that establishes Hilary's materialistic single-mindedness

jokes.text: Come on, Hil, please, at least kiss him first.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks for $500 with no explanation
jokes.punchline: Will suggests she should at least show affection before taking money
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about transactional family relationships

jokes.text: You're right, Uncle Phil, wrong area code. That would be Stacy.
jokes.setup: Will's beeper goes off and he identifies it as Aisha
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself with a different girl's name based on area code
jokes.explanation: Smart joke that shows Will's player mentality and knowledge of his 'territory'

jokes.text: When Jesse Jackson gets a job.
jokes.setup: Ashley asks if she can have a beeper too
jokes.punchline: Philip's impossible condition referencing Jesse Jackson's activism
jokes.explanation: Period-specific political humor that's economical but may not age well

jokes.text: That's because she's your mother and she loves you. I'm your uncle. I just try not to hurt you.
jokes.setup: Will says his mom let him wear the beeper in Philly
jokes.punchline: Philip's honest assessment of their relationship difference
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that's both funny and reveals the uncle-nephew dynamic honestly

jokes.text: And, who are you? Queen Latifah?
jokes.setup: Hilary explains Will's slang terms for women
jokes.punchline: Philip's sarcastic response comparing Hilary to the rapper
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference showing Philip's bewilderment at street slang

jokes.text: That's because you only got one squaw and you all never go nowhere.
jokes.setup: Philip says he doesn't understand Will's dating approach
jokes.punchline: Will's frank assessment of Philip and Vivian's relationship
jokes.explanation: Good generational/lifestyle contrast humor with Will's blunt observation

jokes.text: And, Philip, how come we don't ever go nowhere?
jokes.setup: Will's comment about them never going anywhere
jokes.punchline: Vivian immediately questioning Philip about their social life
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that shows Will stirring up trouble between the couple

jokes.text: The big, fat vein in Daddy's head is starting to throb. / it ain't really serious till his right eye start twitching.
jokes.setup: Philip is getting angry about the beeper situation
jokes.punchline: The family's detailed knowledge of Philip's anger warning signs
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor that shows family dynamics and escalates the physical comedy

jokes.text: it ain't really serious till his right eye start twitching.
jokes.setup: Hilary mentions Philip's throbbing vein
jokes.punchline: Will adds another, more serious anger indicator
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation that builds on Hilary's joke. Shows Will has already learned Philip's anger patterns.

jokes.text: And, you can call me Whitey. / I think not.
jokes.setup: Client introduces himself as Whitey due to white hair
jokes.punchline: Philip's immediate uncomfortable rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward social situation that plays on racial sensitivity with great economy

jokes.text: I think this is our youngest daughter, Ashley. She just had a brain operation. Her head's a little swollen, hence the hat.
jokes.setup: Will introduces Ashley to the party guests
jokes.punchline: Makes up elaborate lie about brain surgery to explain her hat
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor that shows Will's quick thinking and willingness to embarrass family

jokes.text: Switzerland, Vivian. / Not far enough, Philip.
jokes.setup: Ashley appears in inappropriate outfit Will bought her
jokes.punchline: Philip and Vivian's exchange escalating the boarding school joke
jokes.explanation: Strong callback that escalates the running gag with Vivian joining in

jokes.text: Will, you must change. / Carlton, you must grow.
jokes.setup: Vivian tells Will he must change clothes
jokes.punchline: Will's comeback about Carlton's height
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline structure with good parallel construction and character-specific roast

jokes.text: I never liked Will, anyway.
jokes.setup: Ashley is told she can't wear the outfit because it would hurt Will's feelings
jokes.punchline: She immediately throws Will under the bus
jokes.explanation: Perfect young character moment. The immediate betrayal when self-interest is at stake is very believable and funny.

jokes.text: How shall I put this? The food sucks.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey hesitates before giving his opinion on party food
jokes.punchline: Delivers blunt assessment after false politeness buildup
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey moment with perfect setup and character-appropriate bluntness

jokes.text: The pepper's moving.
jokes.setup: Will defends the buffet food that Geoffrey criticized
jokes.punchline: Discovers the food is literally contaminated/moving
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup that validates Geoffrey's harsh criticism with gross-out humor

jokes.text: That is the last time I buy sushi out of the trunk of a car.
jokes.setup: The pepper moving reveals food contamination
jokes.punchline: Hilary's admission about buying sushi from questionable source
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor that perfectly explains the contamination while being character-specific

jokes.text: I would, Aunt Viv, but I wouldn't want to make all your guests jealous.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks Will to take off his clothes
jokes.punchline: Will's cocky assumption about his physical attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Typical Will cockiness humor that fits his character

jokes.text: No, I'm gonna call you that... then you gonna call me the other thing, and we're gonna be here fighting.
jokes.setup: Whitey asks Will to call him by his nickname
jokes.punchline: Will's street-smart prediction of how this interaction will escalate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant social commentary that addresses racial dynamics with Will's street wisdom and humor

jokes.text: Guess a lot of your buddies wearing suits like this now, huh?
jokes.setup: Will learns Whitey runs a savings and loan
jokes.punchline: Implies connection between S&L scandal and prison uniforms
jokes.explanation: Smart topical humor referencing S&L scandals of the era with good character integration

jokes.text: 'Cause everyone knows Buckwheat never went to medical school.
jokes.setup: Will asks why people would think he's a drug dealer instead of doctor
jokes.punchline: Philip's harsh comparison referencing racial stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Problematic humor that relies on dated racial stereotypes, though in character for Philip's frustration

jokes.text: I don't really think that Stacy's your type, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Philip demands Will give him the beeper
jokes.punchline: Will pretends Philip wants it to contact his girls
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Will's ability to deflect serious moments with humor

jokes.text: And what are you looking at, Whitey?
jokes.setup: Will storms out after arguing with Philip
jokes.punchline: Takes out frustration on the innocent client
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that brings back the Whitey situation as Will lashes out

jokes.text: No you won't, worm head.
jokes.setup: Jazz offers to help Will with his bags
jokes.punchline: Will's harsh rejection with creative insult
jokes.explanation: Standard insult comedy that establishes Will's bad mood after leaving Uncle Phil's

jokes.text: You dating Whoopi Goldberg?
jokes.setup: Jazz expected Will to say something else about being back
jokes.punchline: Instead makes joke about Will's hair looking like Whoopi's
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture appearance joke that fits Jazz's character

jokes.text: Dang, your toilet broke, too?
jokes.setup: Will says he doesn't have his nice mansion amenities anymore
jokes.punchline: Jazz focuses on the most basic bathroom function
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shows Jazz's different economic perspective and priorities

jokes.text: Company with breasts. / They're lonely.
jokes.setup: Will asks what company Jazz has over
jokes.punchline: Jazz's blunt and then personified description of his female guests
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate crude humor that establishes Jazz's priorities

jokes.text: Sorry, my brother... but those are the harsh realities of the cruel world in which we habitate. And now, booty awaits me.
jokes.setup: Will appeals to their friendship for a place to stay
jokes.punchline: Jazz's pseudo-philosophical rejection followed by crude motivation
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between pretentious language and base motivation, very Jazz-like

jokes.text: Dang, I thought I was having a bad hair day. You going bald, man. You about to look like my uncle in a minute.
jokes.setup: Will meets the dog owner who appears to be balding
jokes.punchline: Will's observation connecting balding to Uncle Phil
jokes.explanation: Mild callback that maintains Will's roasting style but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: This is a black thing, right? You're going to pee on my foot.
jokes.setup: Dog owner says the dog is his only friend now
jokes.punchline: Will's confused assumption about dog friendship rituals
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor that shows Will's street logic applied to unusual situations

jokes.text: Guess the cat's got its tongue.
jokes.setup: Car alarm fails to activate when Will tries to demonstrate it
jokes.punchline: Will's pun about the 'talking' car being silent
jokes.explanation: Standard pun that works for the situation but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: I've looked everywhere. There isn't an ice cream sandwich left in this town.
jokes.setup: Philip sent Geoffrey out to scour the city looking for Will
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey reveals he was actually looking for ice cream sandwiches
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that perfectly captures Geoffrey's priorities and passive-aggressive nature

jokes.text: Quite right, sir. You threw him on the lawn. He rolled into the street.
jokes.setup: Philip denies throwing Will into the street
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's technical correction with visual detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey deadpan delivery with specific visual that makes the situation funnier

jokes.text: Looks like you really blew it this time, huh, big guy? / I just hope the Willster's still breathing.
jokes.setup: Family realizes Will is missing and in potential trouble
jokes.punchline: Carlton's unhelpful commentary escalating the worry
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate humor showing Carlton's tendency to make situations worse

jokes.text: See how he be trying to play me, Fred?
jokes.setup: Philip asks if there's a problem with Will being brought home by police
jokes.punchline: Will treats the officer as his friend and confidant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's ability to make friends and deflect blame

jokes.text: You forgot the part about Will getting arrested.
jokes.setup: Philip lists all of Will's offenses from the day
jokes.punchline: Carlton helpfully adds the worst item to the list
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - helpful in the worst possible way

jokes.text: Don't joke about that, Will.
jokes.setup: Will asks what if Philip told Carlton he couldn't wear plaid
jokes.punchline: Carlton's serious, horrified reaction to the hypothetical
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reaction that shows Carlton's attachment to his preppy style

jokes.text: When did you have a man in the Jacuzzi?
jokes.setup: Will mentions Hilary entertaining men in the Jacuzzi
jokes.punchline: Philip's delayed horrified realization about his daughter
jokes.explanation: Good callback to opening that shows Phil's parental concern kicking in

jokes.text: That's between me, that man, and his video camera.
jokes.setup: Hilary deflects questions about her private life
jokes.punchline: Adds disturbing detail about video recording
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation that makes the situation worse for Philip

jokes.text: but you don't think a 6'3", 250-pound black dude in a dashiki and an afro the size of West Philly is drawing attention to himself?
jokes.setup: Philip says he was making a cultural statement vs. Will drawing attention
jokes.punchline: Will's detailed description of how conspicuous Philip would have looked
jokes.explanation: Excellent generational argument with vivid imagery that makes a strong logical point

jokes.text: So, as long as you keep the hair... the clothes and the beeper... you're all grounded.
jokes.setup: Philip seems to concede and lets Will keep his style choices
jokes.punchline: Reveals his counter-strategy of punishing the other kids
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that shows Philip's strategic parenting and turns the tables completely

jokes.text: All right, look y'all, this is a classic case of divide and conquer. Look, Malcolm warned us about this. Get him!
jokes.setup: Will realizes Philip has turned his siblings against him
jokes.punchline: References Malcolm X and calls for physical retaliation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that brings in social commentary before devolving into physical comedy

tmdb_overview: Will returns home from spending the summer in Philadelphia with a new look that doesn't sit well with Uncle Phil.
